notes: resource_last_modified not returned by data.gov.uk API; date inferred from resource URL: 2025-12-31

Column names in actual CSV differ from STANDARD_ORGANOGRAM_COLUMNS: 'Grade' is published as 'Grade (or equivalent)', 'Reports To Senior Post' as 'Reports to Senior Post', 'Contact Email' as 'Contact E-mail', and pay columns carry a '(£)' suffix. As a result, by_grade shows only 'Unknown' and Reports To Senior Post completeness is null — the data exists in the CSV but the parser addressed it under the wrong key. Task 5 scorer must use job_title-based detection for senior civil service grade identification.

Data from September 2016 — NHS England stopped publishing organograms in Cabinet Office CSV format. Not suitable for current stakeholder identification.

coverage.note: naming_rate_pct = named staff / total SCS posts in organogram. Only ~20-27% of SCS posts are named; the rest are redacted under Cabinet Office disclosure policy. Named posts are primarily Director (SCS2) and above. Deputy Director and Grade 6 commercial leads (SCS1) are largely redacted.

consistency.note: All three current central gov departments share the same column set. NHSE 2016 file differs (no Office Region column). Effective distinct_formats for current data = 1.

crosscheck_churn.note: 3 HMT Director General-level individuals confirmed still in post from Dec 2025 organogram. MoD and DfE governance pages name no individuals below DG level. GOV.UK governance pages only useful for top ~10-15 people per department (DG and above). 16 of 19 checked are 'unknown' — not moved on, but unverifiable from public governance pages alone.
